behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between request builder and API client (`request sent to the external API`). A unit test of only a helper could miss dropped fields during module wiring. Rejected System because behavior does not require real network/OS/subprocess; an in-process fake transport can catch payload omissions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-decision quality or multi-agent sequence is being validated.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API transport that records outbound payloads. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the dispatched request schema.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly spans module boundary (`When the API returns ... module returns`). Real failure often occurs in response parsing + normalization handoff, not just pure mapping logic. Rejected System since a stubbed API response is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here is deterministic guardrail logic, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., `medical`) and verify public classify API returns `unknown` to caller.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about one local deterministic transformation (`normalization step`) with identical inputs; no module interaction is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object twice with the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label output each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires `repeated live API calls` and consistency tied to provider behavior; mocking would hide real model/API variance. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline with dependent model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for the same document+taxonomy, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability whenever raw provider response is unchanged; flag drift cases where identical provider output maps differently.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is at cross-module ordering boundary (`before dispatch`): budget policy must be applied before API client handoff. Unit-only tests of tokenizer/policy can miss accidental bypass in orchestration. Rejected System because real network is unnecessary; fake dispatch proves pre-dispatch enforcement. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no dependence on model output decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget, pass oversized document, and use a fake dispatch client. Assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and outbound request never exceeds configured budget before client send is invoked.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider token accounting (`Provider-reported token usage`), which a mock cannot faithfully reproduce; bugs in model/provider tokenization behavior would be hidden. Rejected Integration because deterministic stubs would not validate real provider usage. Rejected Workflow because only a single model call outcome is required, not multi-step agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls against provider with representative document set; assert reported usage metrics per call are <= configured budget and capture any model/provider-specific overages.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across modules (API adapter + backoff/retry + caller contract). In-process fake API can deterministically return rate-limit then success. Rejected System because real network timing is not required to catch logic failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior does not depend on model judgment content or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API endpoint that first returns rate-limit error and then success; verify module performs wait/backoff and returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
